mnemonics.title: Pipeline Stages Mnemonic
mnemonics.content: IF-ID-EX-MEM-WB: 'I Find Ice Delivered Extremely Melting While Bathing' - Helps remember Instruction Fetch, Instruction Decode, Execute, Memory, Write-Back stages in order.

mnemonics.title: Hazard Types Mnemonic
mnemonics.content: S.D.C: 'Structural Damages Computers', D.R.A.W: 'Data Reads After Write', C.B: 'Control Branches' - Remembers hazard categories: Structural, Data (RAW/WAR/WAW), Control.

acronyms.usage: True data dependency where instruction reads value written by previous instruction
Anti-dependency where later instruction overwrites data read by earlier instruction (out-of-order execution issue)
Output dependency where multiple instructions write to same location in wrong order
Measure of how many instructions can be executed in parallel in a pipeline
Throughput metric; ideal pipeline achieves IPC = 1 after filling
Dummy instruction inserted as stall/bubble to resolve pipeline hazards

visualTips.visualization: Draw horizontal rows for clock cycles (C1, C2, C3...) and vertical columns for pipeline stages (IF, ID, EX, MEM, WB). Show instruction I1 starting at C1 in IF, moving to ID at C2, etc. Creates diagonal pattern showing instruction overlap.

When hazard occurs, insert empty cells (bubbles) in the timing diagram. Show how bubble propagates through pipeline, blocking subsequent instructions. Bubble appears as blank space moving through stages.
Use rectangular area representation: Non-pipeline = n×k rectangles stacked vertically; Pipeline = same area arranged diagonally. Speedup = vertical height / diagonal length = k (number of stages).

analogies.analogy: Think of car manufacturing assembly line: while one car is being painted, another is being welded, and another is having parts installed. Each station (pipeline stage) does one task repeatedly. This overlap dramatically increases cars produced per hour (throughput), though first car takes time to complete (latency).

Pipeline hazards are like traffic on a single-lane road: structural hazard = road under repair (resource unavailable), data hazard = waiting for car ahead to move (dependency), control hazard = intersection where you must choose direction (branch decision).
A pipeline bubble is like standing in a queue but not being served because the previous person hasn't finished their transaction - you're stalled until the dependency resolves.
